channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Experiencing Meta’s smartglasses: convenience, privacy concerns and social discomfort
item.description: Over the course of a month, the author tested Meta’s smartglasses, featuring an AI assistant voiced by Judi Dench. While the glasses offer functions like taking photos, making calls, giving directions...

item.title: Army recovers abandoned NPA firearms with help of local villagers in Surigao del Sur
item.description: Soldiers of the Philippine Army successfully recovered several firearms abandoned by New People's Army (NPA) guerrillas in Surigao del Sur through coordinated operations aided by vigilant local villag...

item.title: Tanzania Strengthens National Strategy Against Drug-Resistant Pathogens
item.description: Dar es Salaam — Tanzania government imeanza kufanya jitihada kubwa kuzuia pathogens zinazopinga dawa (DR-TDs). Katika kikao cha 32 cha National Coordination Committee on DR-TDs, ambacho kimehusisha wi...

item.title: Different Prayers on Duterte’s Anniversary Highlight Deep Divisions in Philippine Society
item.description: On March 11, the first anniversary of Rodrigo Duterte’s arrest for crimes against humanity, two very different Masses were held in separate cities reflecting contrasting perspectives on his legacy. In...

item.title: Pakistan Stock Exchange KSE-100 index rises sharply amid market recovery
item.description: The Pakistan Stock Exchange (PSX) witnessed a remarkable surge on Wednesday as the KSE-100 index climbed by 7,461.58 points, or 5.02%, reaching 156,204.89 from the previous close of 148,743.31. This s...

item.title: Tanzania strengthens emergency response through national medical team training
item.description: Tanzania dey take strong steps to prepare for health emergencies like pandemics, floods, and other disasters. The government, together with WHO and other partners under the Pandemic Fund, don launch n...

item.title: PNP Introduces Over 5,000 New Equipment and Vehicles to Strengthen Operations
item.description: The Philippine National Police (PNP) recently showcased over 5,000 new pieces of equipment and vehicles aimed at improving police visibility and operational efficiency nationwide. The blessing ceremon...

item.title: Nigeria’s Transport Minister Alkali Steps Down to Contest Gombe Governorship in 2027
item.description: Nigeria’s Minister of Transportation, Saidu Ahmed Alkali, don officially resign from him position to fit pursue Gombe State governorship seat ahead of 2027 general elections. This move follow Presiden...

item.title: Vicky Pattison discusses career pressures and fertility struggles
item.description: Vicky Pattison, widely recognised from Strictly Come Dancing and Geordie Shore, has opened up about the intense pressures she faced within the television industry. Balancing multiple commitments, incl...

item.title: Anthropic discloses internal AI coding files by mistake
item.description: Anthropic, the AI company behind Claude Code, accidentally released part of its internal source code due to human error, the company clarified on Tuesday. The mishap involved an internal-use file that...

item.title: Prominent Peshawar Urologist Hospitalised Following Gun Attack
item.description: In Peshawar, a leading urologist and pioneer in kidney transplants, Dr Asif Malik, sustained serious injuries after being shot by unidentified assailants in the University Town area on Wednesday morni...

item.title: Claims that Murkomen Ordered Matiang'i’s Arrest Proven False
item.description: Recently, social media graphics went viral claiming that Kenya's Interior Minister, Kipchumba Murkomen, had issued an arrest warrant for opposition politician Fred Matiang'i over alleged 'classified c...

item.title: ASX 200 rises despite inflation, energy, and interest rate pressures
item.description: Despite a toxic mix of rising inflation, soaring interest rates, and sharp increases in energy and mortgage costs, the S&P/ASX 200 has only fallen 6 per cent over the past month and surged 1.8 per cen...

item.title: Iranian Rial Sees Sharp Increase Against Pakistani Rupee Amid War and Trade
item.description: KARACHI: The Iranian rial has surged nearly four times in value against the Pakistani rupee amid the ongoing war, primarily due to speculative buying and increased cross-border trade. Market sources r...

item.title: Tiger Woods takes a break for treatment following DUI charge
item.description: Tiger Woods has announced he will take a break to seek treatment and focus on his health after being charged with driving under the influence in Florida. The incident occurred last Friday when Woods, ...

item.title: MCK Invites Final-Year Students for May–July 2026 Media Attachment Programme
item.description: The Media Council of Kenya (MCK) has opened applications for its May–July 2026 industrial attachment programme aimed at journalism and communication students. The programme lasts three months and is d...

item.title: Police Boost Security and Road Safety Measures Across Kenya for Easter
item.description: As Easter holidays approach, the National Police Service (NPS) in Kenya has stepped up security and traffic enforcement nationwide. With schools closing and increased travel, the police are enhancing ...

item.title: Disagreement Over Red Meat and Saturated Fats Between MAHA and American Heart Association
item.description: Recent dietary debates have highlighted a growing divergence between federal nutritional guidelines and the recommendations of established health organizations. The Make America Healthy Again (MAHA) i...

item.title: Bangkok to host first Asian Eurovision Song Contest with 10 countries competing
item.description: The Eurovision Song Contest is set to expand beyond Europe for the first time with its inaugural Asian edition, which will take place in Bangkok, Thailand, this November. At least ten countries are co...

item.title: Canada Prepares Seriously for Bosnia and Herzegovina in World Cup Opener
item.description: As Canada approaches its World Cup opener on home soil against Bosnia and Herzegovina on June 12 at BMO Field, the team is taking the matchup seriously despite Bosnia being ranked 65th in the world. T...
